title: Bridge Essay: Comparative connectomics of the primate social brain.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ritual** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Neural synchrony — interpersonal brain-to-brain coupling, hyperscanning, joint music-making, collective entrainment.

**Comparative connectomics of the primate social brain.**. Neuroimage.. PMID: 34732327. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2021.118693. Social interaction is thought to provide aselection pressure for human intelligence, yet little is known about its neurobiological basis and evolution throughout the primate lineage. Recent advances in neuroimaging have enabled whole brain investigation of brain structure, function, and connectivity in humans and non-human primates (NHPs), leading to anascent field of comparative connectomics. However, linking social behavior to brain organization across the primates remains challenging. Here,
